entities.name: Niche Social Networks
entities.context: Purpose-built platforms oriented around specific interests/communities that V believes will displace generalist social media

entities.name: Generalist Social Networks
entities.context: One-size-fits-all platforms like Facebook that V believes are losing dominance

entities.name: Facebook
entities.context: Referenced as a canonical example of a generalist social platform being displaced

entities.name: Purpose-Built Communities
entities.context: V's term for the future of social — communities designed around specific value propositions

entities.name: Platform Displacement Theory
entities.context: V's worldview position that niche platforms will increasingly displace generalist ones

entities.name: Authentic Connection
entities.context: A core user desire that V sees niche platforms fulfilling better than generalists

entities.name: Privacy-Savvy Younger Users
entities.context: Demographic trend V identifies — younger users prefer ethical companies and split engagement across specialized platforms

entities.name: Ethical Tech Companies
entities.context: V notes younger users prefer companies with ethical stances, favoring niche over generalist platforms

relationships.context: V believes niche platforms will increasingly displace generalist ones
Facebook cited as canonical example of a generalist platform losing ground
Niche platforms embody the purpose-built community model V sees as the future
Niche platforms better serve users' desire for authentic connection
Younger users' privacy awareness and preference for ethical companies drives adoption of niche platforms
Younger demographic prefers companies with clear ethical stances
V holds as a worldview position that the era of generalist social media dominance is ending
